rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into implementation scope when bindings depend on subcomponents or connections visible only at implementation level.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL structure by only referencing ports, components, and types explicitly defined in the source model.
Place input/output range constraints in component type scope if they use visible ports; use implementation scope only for implementation-specific bindings.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` for all AGREE contracts.
Ensure all variables in AGREE expressions are mapped to AADL ports or constants explicitly declared in scope.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables.
Do not invent numeric bounds (e.g., x >= 0) not explicitly stated in the requirement.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
